title: Shri Jagannath Ji Ki Aarti
intro: Read Shri Jagannath Ji Ki Aarti in Hindi and seek the divine blessings of Lord Jagannath Ji.
description:
Om Jai Jagannath Hare, Swami! Jai Jagannath Hare.
Bhaktan ke hitkari, bhaktan ke hitkari,
Kasht sada hi tare॥
॥ Om Jai Jagannath Hare ॥

Ratna sinhasan sundar, dhur-dhur chhavi chhai.
Sur-muni jan ki mohan, sur-muni jan ki mohan,
Sabki sudh bisrai॥
॥ Om Jai Jagannath Hare ॥

Brahmadik Shiv Narad, karat stuti bhari.
Bhaktan ki peeda harne, bhaktan ki peeda harne,
Mahima hai tumhari॥
॥ Om Jai Jagannath Hare ॥

Kamal nayan chaturbhuj, pitambar sohe.
Garud vahan par baithe, garud vahan par baithe,
Dekhat man mohe॥
॥ Om Jai Jagannath Hare ॥

Jagannath Swami ji ki, aarti jo koi gaave.
Prem sahit man bhaave, prem sahit man bhaave,
Vanchit phal paave॥
॥ Om Jai Jagannath Hare ॥

faq.q1: What is the main significance of performing Shri Jagannath Ji's Aarti?
faq.a1: It is performed to seek spiritual liberation (Moksha), purification of the mind, and the blessings of the Lord of the Universe.
faq.q2: Which is the most auspicious day for his worship?
faq.a2: The day of 'Rath Yatra' and every Ekadashi or Purnima (full moon) are considered highly auspicious for him.
faq.q3: What offerings (Prasad) are preferred for Lord Jagannath during Aarti?
faq.a3: Lord Jagannath is very fond of 'Mahaprasad' (rice, dal, vegetable curry) and various types of sweets.
faq.q4: Can Shri Jagannath Ji's Aarti be performed at home?
faq.a4: Yes, with full devotion and purity, one can perform the Aarti of Lord Jagannath at home.
faq.q5: What should be done after the Aarti for peace of mind?
faq.a5: Reciting the 'Jagannath Ashtakam' after the Aarti is considered the best practice for peace of mind.
